source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Turn end via terminal text
notes: Smallest tool-less round-trip pinning the terminal-text end signal: one user prompt, one assistant reply with no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc and final ready. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE aider driver (a `send`/`wait_turn`/`sleep` script in a fresh per-run git cwd) so the process outlives the daemon's observation window. Aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD, RawLineParser), so the daemon keys the whole arc to a single proc-<PID> row — no transcript-UUID rollout phase. SCOPE NOTE (the assessed observes:partial carve-out): aider's markdown transcript carries NO end-of-turn token — there is no stop_reason / task_complete / structured terminal signal (structured_output_jsonl=false). The `> Tokens:` line that closes each model call is explicitly NOT treated as end-of-turn (the parser keeps turnOpen true because --yes-always may re-prompt). The named terminal-token mechanism this cell pins is therefore ABSENT for aider; irrlicht synthesizes turn_done out-of-band via Parser.IdleFlush after ~1.5s of transcript quiescence, clamped to the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll, so the working→ready edge lands with ~5s effective latency. The final state IS correct (ready) — the gap is the absent in-band token plus the ~5s lag, not a wrong end state, so the arc is still asserted. turn_end_terminal anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready. The tool_calls_max:0 invariant is expressed as a `no tool_use` negative assertion on the turn window. This spec is the DOCUMENTED TARGET; recording is deferred (no aider auth in this environment).

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Terminal assistant text ends the turn → back to ready (synthesized by IdleFlush, no in-band terminal token, ~5s lag); no tool call in the turn; state never entered waiting
